criteria.text: Log360 server is running with a dedicated service account (not LocalSystem or Administrator)
criteria.recommendation: Configure Log360 to run under a dedicated domain service account with minimum permissions. The service account needs: Log on as a service right, read access to integrated component APIs, and read access to SIEM data sources. Avoid Domain Admin membership.

criteria.text: Log360 is on the latest available build (13000 series)
criteria.recommendation: Update to the latest 13000-series build immediately. This series introduces major capabilities: AI Alert Investigation Agent, Zia AI Assistant, native SOAR, re-architected detection engine with 1,500+ MITRE ATT&CK rules, native UEBA integration, and Access Gateway Clusters. Check Admin > Product Updates.

criteria.text: Log360 server meets hardware requirements for integrated suite deployment
criteria.recommendation: Log360 integrates multiple components (EventLog Analyzer, ADAudit Plus, DataSecurity Plus) on the same platform. Ensure dedicated hardware: minimum 32GB RAM, 12 CPU cores, 1TB SSD for production. Log360 is the central nervous system of your security monitoring—inadequate resources cause event drops and alerting failures.

criteria.text: External database (MSSQL/PostgreSQL) is configured for production
criteria.recommendation: Use an external MSSQL or PostgreSQL database for all production Log360 deployments. External databases support HA, enterprise backup, and better query performance. Bundled databases are not suitable for enterprise-scale deployments. Configure under Admin > Database.

criteria.text: High Availability (HA) is configured for the Log360 platform
criteria.recommendation: Configure Log360 HA to prevent single points of failure in your primary SIEM. HA failover should be tested quarterly. For distributed deployments, consider Active/Passive HA with automatic failover. A failed SIEM during an active incident is a critical operational failure.

criteria.text: Log360 license includes all required component modules (ELA, ADAudit, DataSecurity Plus)
criteria.recommendation: Verify all required component licenses are active: EventLog Analyzer (log count), ADAudit Plus (DC count), DataSecurity Plus (file server count). Expired component licenses silently stop collection for that data source. Navigate to Admin > License to verify.

criteria.text: Re-architected detection engine (13000 series) is active with central detection console
criteria.recommendation: Verify the new detection engine in the 13000 series is fully operational. The re-architected engine provides: central detection console, object-level filters, cloud-delivered rule updates, and 1,500+ MITRE ATT&CK-aligned detection rules. Navigate to Detection > Detection Console to verify status.

criteria.text: Custom detection rules are created for organization-specific threat models
criteria.recommendation: Supplement cloud-delivered rules with custom detection rules based on your specific threat model: insider threat scenarios, supply chain attack patterns, environment-specific attack paths. Use the rule builder to create rules without writing code. Document each custom rule's threat hypothesis.

criteria.text: Centralized integration management is configured for all connected products
criteria.recommendation: Use Log360's Centralized Integration Management console to manage all product integrations from a single interface. Verify all integration health statuses are green. Configure alerts for integration health failures to detect when an integration silently stops working. Navigate to Admin > Integrations > Integration Health.

criteria.text: NetFlow Analyzer integration is configured for network traffic visibility
criteria.recommendation: Configure the native NetFlow Analyzer integration to add network flow data to Log360 SIEM. NetFlow data enables: detection of data exfiltration by volume, C2 beaconing patterns, lateral movement by connection patterns, and DNS tunneling. Navigate to Admin > Integrations > ManageEngine > NetFlow Analyzer.

criteria.text: Firewall Analyzer integration is configured for firewall log correlation
criteria.recommendation: Configure the native Firewall Analyzer integration to forward firewall audit logs to Log360. This enables cross-product correlation: a firewall policy change correlated with failed VPN authentication attempts points to an attacker attempting to disable perimeter controls. Navigate to Admin > Integrations > ManageEngine > Firewall Analyzer.

criteria.text: OpManager network monitoring integration is configured for operational context
criteria.recommendation: Configure OpManager audit log forwarding to Log360. Network device operational events (interface down, device unreachable, configuration change) provide context that distinguishes security incidents from operational issues. Correlate OpManager events with security events for better incident classification.

criteria.text: ServiceDesk Plus / ITSM integration is configured for automated incident ticket creation
criteria.recommendation: Configure bidirectional ITSM integration: Log360 auto-creates incident tickets for high-severity alerts, and resolved tickets automatically close Log360 incidents. This creates an accountable incident management process and provides audit evidence of response activities. Navigate to Admin > Integrations > ITSM.

criteria.text: Incident workflow is defined with severity levels, assignment rules, and SLAs
criteria.recommendation: Define incident management workflows: severity classification (P1-P4), auto-assignment rules (route cloud incidents to cloud team), SLA targets (P1: 1hr, P2: 4hr, P3: 24hr, P4: 72hr), and escalation procedures. Navigate to Incidents > Workflow Configuration.

criteria.text: Incident timeline and evidence documentation is used for all security incidents
criteria.recommendation: Train analysts to use Log360's incident timeline feature to document: timeline of events, affected systems, actions taken, evidence collected, and post-incident findings. This documentation is required for regulatory reporting (GDPR breach notification, PCI incident response documentation).

criteria.text: Expanded compliance packs are deployed for applicable frameworks
criteria.recommendation: Deploy all applicable compliance packs from the Log360 marketplace: PCI-DSS, HIPAA, SOX, GDPR, ISO 27001, NIST CSF, FISMA, CCPA, and industry-specific frameworks. Compliance packs provide pre-built dashboards, reports, and alert rules mapped to control requirements. Navigate to Compliance > Marketplace.

criteria.text: Compliance dashboard shows current compliance posture for all frameworks
criteria.recommendation: Configure the compliance dashboard to show real-time compliance posture for all applicable frameworks. Schedule automated compliance reports for delivery to GRC team, auditors, and CISO. Use the compliance gap analysis to prioritize remediation efforts.

criteria.text: HTTPS / TLS 1.2+ is enforced with valid SSL certificate and weak ciphers disabled
criteria.recommendation: Enforce HTTPS-only access to Log360 console. Configure TLS 1.2+ with strong cipher suites (AES-256, SHA-256). Disable: TLS 1.0, TLS 1.1, SSLv3, RC4, DES, 3DES, NULL ciphers, EXPORT ciphers. Install a certificate from your enterprise CA. Configure HTTP to HTTPS redirect.

criteria.text: Two-factor authentication is mandatory for all Log360 administrator accounts
criteria.recommendation: Enforce 2FA for all Log360 accounts, especially administrators. Log360 provides visibility into your entire security posture—a compromised admin account would allow attackers to disable alerts, delete evidence, and view your detection capabilities. Enable under Admin > Security > MFA.

criteria.text: Product activity audit log is enabled and protected from modification
criteria.recommendation: Enable and protect the Log360 product activity audit log. This log must capture: who logged in, what reports/queries they ran, what configuration changes were made, and what data was exported. This is critical for detecting insider misuse of the SIEM and maintaining audit chain of custody.

criteria.text: Log collection failure alerting is configured for critical log sources
criteria.recommendation: Configure alerts for log collection failures on critical sources (DCs, firewalls, WAF, critical servers). A silent log source failure creates a security blind spot—attackers know to target systems that aren't being monitored. Set threshold: alert if source silent >15 minutes for critical, >60 minutes for standard sources.
